commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Codex app-server context projection to tail-truncate oversized rendered context, adds a regression test, and adds a changelog fix entry.

Reproducibility: yes. for the focused source path: current main front-truncates chronological rendered context, and the PR includes a before/after overflow regression. The live Telegram/OpenClaw channel path is not yet reproduced in posted proof.
commands.review_followup: Contributor or maintainer follow-up is needed for redacted real behavior proof; there is no narrow automated code repair to request.

target.title: Fix recent context loss in long Codex sessions
target.body:
## Summary

- Preserve all rendered Codex app-server context-engine history when it fits the projection budget.
- When projected history is too large and must be clipped, truncate from the older side so the newest turns survive.
- Add regression coverage for a long stale session followed by recent recrawl/Codex CLI context, plus an Unreleased changelog entry.

## Root Cause

Codex app-server context-engine projection rendered assembled conversation context in chronological order and then truncated the whole rendered block from the front. That clipping was not an intentional compaction boundary; it was a projection-size guard. In long Telegram topic sessions, the guard preserved stale previous-day context while silently dropping the recent user turns and native Codex CLI activity that the follow-up depended on.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Short follow-ups in long Codex-backed channel sessions should keep the session context that fits, and if projected history exceeds the Codex prompt budget, the newest assembled turns must be retained instead of being clipped away.
Real environment tested: Temp OpenClaw worktrees: detached `origin/main` at `833f1ce735150ac7a13a9b9eac80b19e9f8d96e6` for the before-side repro, and PR branch `telegram-short-followup-context-fix` at `68f3c80ec05d4547c984df77d19d45bdfdbd5957` for the after-side proof.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/context-engine-projection.test.ts -t "keeps recent context when the rendered conversation overflows"`
Evidence after fix: `Test Files 1 passed (1)` and `Tests 1 passed | 12 skipped (13)` on the PR branch; the full focused suite also returned `Test Files 2 passed (2)` and `Tests 26 passed (26)`.
Observed result after fix: The regression keeps `create recrawl` and `codex exec -C /tmp/recrawl started` in the projected context while omitting only older overflow text.
What was not tested: Live Telegram replay and full CI were not run locally; this PR exercises the focused app-server projection path that produced the bad prompt.

## Before/after proof

Before fix on detached `origin/main` at `833f1ce735150ac7a13a9b9eac80b19e9f8d96e6`:

- Command: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/context-engine-projection.context-loss-proof.test.ts -t "keeps recent context when the rendered conversation overflows"`
- Result: `Test Files 1 failed (1)` and `Tests 1 failed (1)`.
- Evidence: the repro failed with `AssertionError: expected 'OpenClaw assembled context for this t…' not to contain 'old discrawl setup from previous day'`; the received prompt retained the old Discrawl setup and stale filler, then ended with `[truncated 11425 chars]` before the recent recrawl/Codex CLI turns.

After fix on `telegram-short-followup-context-fix` at `68f3c80ec05d4547c984df77d19d45bdfdbd5957`:

- Command: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/context-engine-projection.test.ts -t "keeps recent context when the rendered conversation overflows"`
- Result: `Test Files 1 passed (1)` and `Tests 1 passed | 12 skipped (13)`.
- Evidence: the regression asserts the projected prompt keeps `create recrawl`, keeps `codex exec -C /tmp/recrawl started`, drops the old Discrawl overflow marker text, and still includes `Current user request:\n?`.

## Verification

- `pnpm install`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/context-engine-projection.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.context-engine.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `~/.codex/skills/codex-review/scripts/codex-review --mode branch --parallel-tests "node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/context-engine-projection.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.context-engine.test.ts"` - `codex-review clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported`

## What was not tested

- Live Telegram topic replay against the original private topic.
- Full repository CI.
